name: infUSion Magazine
summary: InfUSion Magazine is a semesterly, student produced publication that covers the latest campus, local, state and national news. InfUSion is the first and only multicultural  magazine on the University of Georgia's campus.

name: University Judiciary
summary: University Judiciary is a student-run organization that helps students who are charged with violating the Code of Conduct in the Conduct Process. UJ also proactively educates students about the Conduct Process and provides support through sanctioning.

name: Georgia Association for Women Lawyers – University of Georgia Chapter
summary: The Georgia Association for Women Lawyers (GAWL) at the University of Georgia exists to serve the women within the law school by providing mentoring, networking, and other resources to its members and the law school community.

name: Shop with a Bulldog
summary: SWAB is an organization that strives to celebrate the value of community, the excitement of the holidays, and the children of Athens! We aim to grow every year in our relations within the community and across campus to further our mission.

name: University Union Student Programming Board
summary: University Union hosts a wide variety of events ranging from comedy shows, festivals, classic film experiences, and late night programming. Most importantly, all of the Union programs are selected, promoted, and produced for students by students.

name: EcoReach
summary: EcoReach is an environmental education outreach group based out of the Odum School of Ecology. We develop and present environmental- and science-based lessons to local K-12 students and community members during Fall and Spring semesters.

name: Science Olympiad at UGA
summary: Science Olympiad at UGA works to promote the STEAM fields in the local community while also organizing a Science Olympiad tournament each year.

name: Plant Biology Graduate Students Association
summary: The Plant Biology Graduate Students Association (PBGSA) is an association to coordinate and manage the affairs of graduate students in the UGA Department of Plant Biology.

name: Linguistics Society at UGA
summary: Linguistics Society at UGA is an organization that consists of graduate students in the UGA Department of Linguistics. We plan and support linguistics-related, academic, and social events.

name: Residence Hall Association
summary: The Residence Hall Association (RHA) focuses on creating communities within residence halls for students living on campus and on advocating on behalf of all residents through various initiatives and programs throughout campus.

name: Relay For Life at UGA
summary: Relay for Life is a program that benefits the American Cancer Society. We celebrate, remember, and fight back to honor and support cancer survivors & caregivers. Through community and campus events, we hope to fundraise, educate, and advocate for a cure.

name: Future Health Promoters at UGA
summary: Open to all majors, we are committed to raising health awareness and supporting causes that enhance the health and well-being of UGA and Athens communities. We do this through tabling, community service, fitness classes, and fundraising events

name: Students for Life
summary: Students for Life works to educate fellow students about the loss of life due to abortion and to promote awareness of resources for pregnant and parenting students.

name: American Society of Heating, Refrigeration, and Air-Conditioning Engineers
summary: ASHRAE, founded in 1894, is a global society advancing human well-being through sustainable technology for the built environment. This is the UGA student chapter of the Society.

name: Human Development and Family Sciences Graduate Student Organization
summary: HDFS GSO (or GSO for short) is a collection of student scholars who are focused on engendering the professional development of students who are interested in families.

name: Leukemia and Lymphoma Society Club
summary: The Leukemia and Lymphoma Society Club raises funds and awareness for blood cancer research and treatments for patients.

name: Integrative Conservation Student Organization
summary: The Integrative Conservation Student Organization serves the students of the Integrative Conservation (ICON) program by creating spaces for the exchange of ideas among students and conservation thinkers at UGA and beyond.

name: Plant Breeding Genetics and Genomics Graduate Student Association
summary: The PBGG GSA exists to provide a framework for developing professional, academic, and social networks and to foster an inclusive environment for graduate students, in recognition of the importance of positive peer relationships.

name: Beta Alpha Psi
summary: The primary objective of Beta Alpha Psi is to encourage and give recognition to scholastic and professional excellence in the business information field.

name: Geology Club at UGA
summary: The Geology Club is a professional organization dedicated to furthering one's knowledge of geology and the world around us. The Geology Club focuses on fun enriching trips, outreach opportunities, and educational talks.

name: Ecology Graduate Student Organization
summary: GSO is an advocacy organization which serves as a representative and decision-making body for the Ecology graduate student community and provides a unified voice to faculty and staff within the department and to the greater campus community.

name: Ecotones @ UGA
summary: The Ecotones are the only ecologically minded a cappella group on campus. We're a co-ed group that brings our love of music and the environment together. Check us out on Facebook and Instagram to see when our auditions and shows are!

name: Dean William Tate Honor Society
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to recognize the top 24 first-year students each year who have made a contribution to the University of Georgia community through their academic excellence and extracurricular involvement.

name: Poultry Science Club
summary: The poultry science club is a club consisting of students who are interested in the poultry industry or birds in general. Meeting consist of a variety of things such as guest speakers, philanthropy, or fun activities to help students, others, and faculty

name: Alumni and Associates of Family, Career, & Community Leaders of America
summary: A professional organization for all majors within the College of Family and Consumer Sciences that supports middle and high school chapters of Family, Career, and Community Leaders of America (FCCLA).

name: Georgia Museum of Art Student Association, The
summary: The Georgia Museum of Art Student Association is committed to promoting the museum to students and encouraging them to be involved in the arts at UGA and in Athens. We do this by hosting Student Nights, holding meetings, & organizing community art events

name: Classic City Jazz
summary: A student run vocal jazz group known for our close-knit members, tight harmonies, casual atmosphere, and beautiful music. We sing a cappella as well as accompanied pieces when we perform at private events, public concerts, jazz jams, and more.

name: Whatever It Takes at UGA
summary: We are a student run initiative to build stronger communities in Athens. Through an after school elementary tutoring program, and special events within the community, we are striving to give back to Athens-Clarke county.

name: Wildlife Society, The
summary: The student chapter of The Wildlife Society at UGA promotes conservation and responsible management of natural resources while supporting members in becoming certified wildlife biologists through education, outreach, fieldwork, and career development.

name: Kendo
summary: Our mission, at Kendo at UGA, is to cultivate the understanding, promote the tradition, and exercise our minds and bodies in The Way of the Sword.

name: Ice Hockey Club at UGA
summary: The UGA Hockey team is formed by a collection of former AA, AAA, High School and Junior players playing hockey while attending the University of Georgia.

name: Cellular Biology Graduate Student Association
summary: CBGSA builds a sense of community among the graduate students and faculty of the Cellular Biology Department.

name: First Page at UGA
summary: First Page at UGA seeks to close the literacy gap in Athens-Clarke County by providing free books to low-income students. We do so by volunteering in the local community and fundraising money for our grant program.

name: Graduate and Professional Scholars
summary: GAPS is a student-run organization dedicated to serving the needs of historically underrepresented graduate and professional students at the University of Georgia.

name: Model United Nations
summary: Model UN is an interactive crisis simulation of the United Nations and other bodies. We challenge students to learn about international diplomacy through competitions across North America and the two conferences UGA Model UN hosts on-campus.

name: Thalian Blackfriars, The
summary: The Thalian Blackfriars is a student run theatre company at the University of Georgia that has been active on campus since 1893! Today TBF produces several shows a year of both original and published works.

name: American Society of Interior Designers at UGA
summary: ASID is a community of people —designers, industry representatives, educators and students— committed to interior design. We emphasize education, knowledge sharing, advocacy, community building and outreach to advance the interior design profession.

name: Warnell Graduate Student Association
summary: WGSA is an advocacy and service organization composed of all graduate, post-baccalaureate, and professional students at the Warnell School of Forestry and Natural Resources.

name: Association of School Psychologists
summary: To advise the scholarly goals and professional interests of future school psychologists.

name: Minority Student Science Association
summary: MSSA serves as a resource to support the academic and professional careers of all students interested in pursuing pre-health and science-based career paths. We provide several opportunities that better prepare our members to succeed.

name: Georgia Debate Union
summary: The purpose of the Georgia Debate Union is to provide students of all levels with the opportunity to engage in intercollegiate debate, to attend tournaments throughout the U.S.A., and to carry on the research associated with a competitive debate program.

name: Russian Language Club
summary: Club dedicated to teaching non-Russians and Russians alike about culture, customs, and way of life of the Russian speaking world.

name: Business Law Society
summary: The Business Law Society was formed, and continues to exist, to help bring together law students interested in corporate and business law for educational and professional development.

name: American Society of Mechanical Engineers
summary: ASME’s mission is to serve diverse global communities by advancing, disseminating and applying engineering knowledge for improving the quality of life; and communicating the excitement of engineering.

name: Stethoscope Magazine
summary: We are a student-run magazine at the University of Georgia dedicated to promoting science and providing relevant healthcare news.

name: Industrial-Organizational Psychology Student Association
summary: Association for graduate students in the I-O Psychology program at UGA

name: Language and Literacy Education Graduate Organization
summary: LLEGO provides professional development and social opportunities to faculty, staff, and graduate students in the Language and Literacy Education department.

name: Caribbean Student Association
summary: The purpose of the Caribbean Student Association is to promote awareness of the Caribbean culture on the University of Georgia campus.

name: Thomas Lay After-School Program
summary: The Thomas Lay After School Program serves elementary and middle school students of the Athens area. Every weekday, UGA students come to volunteer as tutors and mentors. This program creates an atmosphere that encourages enthusiastic learning and play.

name: Black Affairs Council
summary: An organization created to Preserve, Enhance, Examine, and Celebrate African-American and other minority culture on UGA's campus and in the Athens-Clarke County community.

name: Student Composers Association
summary: The Student Composers Association is an organization of students and faculty members who are interested in composing music. SCA supports and encourages creating, researching, and performing new music.

name: Genetics Graduate Student Association
summary: The purpose of the GGSA shall be to provide intra-departmental and university-wide representation for genetics graduate students and foster scientific communication between current genetics graduate students and prominent scientists in the field.

name: Society of Women Engineers
summary: Our mission is to encourage all students—while honoring our legacy of supporting women—to achieve their full potential in careers as engineers and leaders, expanding the image of the engineering profession as a positive force.

name: Black Male Leadership Society
summary: BMLS fosters unity and growth among Black males while uplifting the broader community. Though centered on Black men’s experiences, membership is open to all UGA students who support the mission and values of the organization.

name: Chapel Bell, The: Positive Press Publication
summary: By creating and distributing a unique, campus-wide publication, we aim to inspire students toward action and positivity on campus and beyond.

name: American Society of Agricultural and Biological Engineers
summary: The goal of this society is to promote directly, and indirectly, the interest of the students involved with Agricultural and Biological engineering, namely those related to professional development and the parent society of ASABE.

name: College Student Affairs Administration Graduate Association
summary: The College Student Affairs Administration Student Association (CSAAGA) is a student-led organization that serves as a social and educational group for students interested in student affairs, faculty, and staff, and represents the program across campus.

name: HEROs at UGA
summary: UGA HEROs strives to improve the quality of life for kids in Georgia affected by HIV/AIDS through enriching programs, unforgettable experiences, and connections to the community.

name: Cattlemen's Association
summary:
The mission of the UGA Cattlemen's Association is to unite cattle producers to advance the economic,
political, and social interests of Georgia's cattle industry while being an affiliate of the Georgia Cattlemen's Association.

name: Workforce Education and Instructional Technology Graduate Association
summary: Workforce Education and Instructional Technology Graduate Association (WEITGA), a graduate association committed to academic engagement, professional development, and collaborative partnerships among students, faculty, and community members.

name: Microbiology Graduate Student Association
summary: A group dedicated to improving the lives of microbiology graduate students through on-campus events, seminar series, and social happenings.

name: National Kitchen and Bath Association Student Chapter
summary: NKBA Student Chapter strives to enhance member success in the design field, promote professionalism and ethical business practices, and provide leadership and direction for the kitchen and bath/interior design industry.

name: National Student Speech Language Hearing Association at UGA
summary: The National Student Speech Language Hearing Association at UGA is the only official national student association recognized by our field’s national organization, ASHA (American Speech-Language-Hearing Association).

name: Grady Graduate Caucus
summary: The social and academic organization of all current Master's and Ph.D students attending the Grady College of Journalism and Mass Communication.

name: Student Alumni Council
summary: The mission of SAC is to enhance the collegiate experience of current UGA students by providing programs and events focused on promoting university traditions, fostering a culture of donorship amongst students, and connecting students to UGA alumni.

name: Social and Personality Psychology at UGA
summary: Social and Personality Psychology at UGA (SPPUGA) is an organization of graduate students interested in learning and discussing past and current issues and topics in the field of Social/Personality Psychology.

name: Society of Environmental Engineers
summary: This organization fosters a network of members and alumni eager to enact positive change in our community, the environment, and the world.

name: Black Theatrical Ensemble, The
summary: BTE creates an opportunity for students interested in drama to showcase their talents while exposing members and audiences to the works of African American playwrights. Membership is open to all and does not require a theatrical background!

name: Real Estate Society
summary: To promote interest in the field of real estate; to promote academic study in the field of real estate; to encourage internal fellowship among students and professionals; to assist Terry real estate students in finding real estate jobs.

name: Students for Latinx Empowerment
summary: We strive to promote higher education to Latinx high school students in Georgia. Our endgame is that more Latinx students attend and succeed at the University of Georgia.

name: Economics Society
summary: We exist to add value to the lives of students at the University of Georgia through 3 primary avenues: academic lectures (human capital), professional development (economic capital), and cultivating a community around economics (social/cultural capital).

name: ASL Dawgs
summary: ASL Dawgs strives to provide educational opportunities for all students interested in American Sign Language and Deaf culture by providing social programming for students centered around American Sign Language as well as exposure to Deaf culture.

name: Psi Chi the National Honor Society in Psychology
summary: Psi Chi is an international honor society whose purpose shall be to encourage, stimulate, and maintain excellence in scholarship of the individual members in all fields, particularly in psychology, and to advance the science of psychology.

name: With Someone Else's Money A Cappella
summary: WSEM is the oldest mixed-voice a cappella group on UGA’s campus. We pride ourselves on inclusivity, fun, and, of course, our music. Check us out on Instagram to access the linktree on our bio or and feel free to message with any questions! @ugawsem

name: Graduate Researchers in Educational Psychology
summary: GREP is designed to foster contacts and communication between students and faculty in the Department of Educational Psychology and to encourage active participation in projects relating to the various fields represented in the department.

name: Culture Club: Critical Media Studies Interest Group
summary: Forum for exploring critical approaches to media study.

name: Bag the Bag
summary: Bag the Bag works on campus and in the ACC community to reduce plastic waste through education, political advocacy, and recycling. We advocate for a more responsible and sustainable community that works together to change consumer habits inour society.

name: UNICEF at UGA
summary: We work to save children’s lives and defend their rights by fundraising, educating, and advocating on behalf of the United Nations Children's Fund.

name: Musicology/Ethnomusicology Student Association
summary: This organization aims to provide both academic and social opportunities for students interested in musical scholarship. We host speakers, an annual symposium, and other various social events throughout the year.

name: Marine Science Graduate Student Association
summary: The Marine Sciences Graduate Student Association organizes social and outreach activities, and articulates students' needs and concerns within the Department.

name: Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity, International Pre-Law Chapter
summary: Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity, International is a professional law fraternity advancing integrity, compassion and courage through service to the student, the school, the profession and the community.

name: Collegiate 4-H
summary: Collegiate 4-H at the University of Georgia strives to serve UGA and surrounding communities through our service, leadership, and dedication to 4-H and its members by extending 4-H involvement at local, district, state, and national levels.

name: Student Affiliates of the American Chemical Society
summary: Chem club hosts monthly to monthly meetings for chemistry students and students of all other majors and provides many opportunities for involvement in academia. Join us as we learn and have a good time together as we stay positive (like protons)!

name: American Society for Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing - UGA Student Chapter
summary: The UGA-ASPRS Student Chapter strives to advance knowledge and improve understanding of mapping sciences and the responsible applications of geospatial technology through events and activities at UGA.

name: Mathematics Education Student Association
summary: The Mathematics Education Student Association is a professional organization of persons individually and collectively dedicated to the improvement of mathematics education at all levels.

name: International Justice Mission at UGA
summary: As one of the largest anti-slavery organizations in the world, IJM is committed to ending slavery in this generation. Our UGA campus club helps by fundraising, raising awareness, and volunteering at local anti-slavery agencies. We're in it to end it!

name: Math Club
summary: We are a student organization at UGA for people of ALL majors interested in mathematics. We host regular talks by undergrads, grad students, faculty, and visiting professors on interesting topics and hold social events to meet other math enthusiasts.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
summary: UGA ASCE is devoted to developing industry-ready professional civil engineers through exposure to real-life engineering applications, networking opportunities, community service, engineering competitions, and internal relationship-building events.

name: American Meteorological Society and National Weather Association at UGA
summary: Our purpose is to give students and faculty alike the opportunity to discuss and enjoy the weather and atmospheric sciences outside of a classroom setting by sponsoring speakers, field trips, and social events.

name: Campus Girl Scouts at UGA
summary: An outreach initiative offering underserved girls at locations throughout Athens the opportunity to experience Girl Scouting; here's to Samoas and self-confidence.

name: National Association for the Advancement of Colored People
summary: The mission of the National Association for the Advancement of Colored People is to ensure the political, educational, social, and economic equality of rights of all persons and to eliminate race-based discrimination.

name: Human Development and Family Science Association
summary: HDFSA is an undergraduate student organization for students majoring in or interested in Human Development and Family Science. We focus on professional development, community service, and personal growth.

name: Hispanic Student Association
summary: The Hispanic Student Association (HSA) of the UGA aims to support the needs of the Latinx student body, and to promote the cultural diversity of the Hispanic/Latinx culture. HSA is an all inclusive organization where all students are welcome!

name: Graduate Student Association
summary: The Graduate Student Association (GSA) is the primary advocacy and social body for graduate students. Its Graduate Student Council consists of representatives elected from departments across campus who voice the needs of their fellow graduate students.

name: Hugh Hodgson Singers
summary: The UGA Hodgson Singers serves as the flagship choral ensemble of the Hugh Hodgson School of Music at the University of Georgia, representing undergraduate through doctoral students of all disciplines.

name: Designated Dawgs
summary: Designated Dawgs is a student-run, 501(c)3 non-profit that provides free, safe, non-judgmental rides home for University of Georgia students.

name: Arts Collaborative
summary: The Arts Collaborative student organization is a network of undergraduate and graduate students interested in interdisciplinary arts collaboration.

name: Semper Fi Society
summary: Our club is designed to train, educate, and mentor United States Marine Corps officer candidates in the process to becoming a Marine Corps officer.

name: Graphic Design Club
summary: The Graphic Design Club is made up of student designers that work to build a strong design community and creative professional connections in the Athens and Atlanta, Georgia.

name: Women's Ultimate Frisbee
summary: We are the Women's Club Ultimate Frisbee team

name: Communication Studies Graduate Forum
summary: The CSGF promotes communication, educational opportunities, and scholarly activity among graduate students in the Department of Communication Studies.

name: Lifelong Education, Administration, and Policy Graduate Student Network
summary: LEAP GSN facilitates social and professional interaction among graduate students in the Department of Lifelong Education, Administration, and Policy within UGA's College of Education. We provide learners with a communication network and a support system.

name: Family Law Society
summary: A student organization focusing on the various careers and opportunities in the area of family and domestic law.

name: Finance Society
summary: Finance Society is a business organization that provides its members with valuable information and contacts. Its goals are to support communications between the academic and business worlds while fostering network opportunities for students.

name: School of Public and International Affairs Student Union
summary: We aim to build a sense of community and provide opportunities for networking and career development to SPIA students at UGA.

name: Georgia Daze Minority Recruitment Program
summary: Georgia Daze aims to encourage talented, ambitious and innovative students to enroll at UGA, while working to provide programs and opportunities for current UGA students to bridge the University-at-large population and our community!

name: COMPASS
summary: The Comparative Literature and Intercultural Studies Graduate Student Organization at UGA

name: Stillpoint Literary Magazine
summary: Stillpoint Literary Magazine is a student-run literary magazine that annually publishes poetry, prose, and visual arts in print and online. The organization also hosts literary events and fundraisers throughout the year.

name: Public Interest Law Council
summary: PILC is dedicated to the promotion of service of the public interest, civilly and criminally, at the University of Georgia School of Law. We hope to foster involvement and interest in every area of public interest law.

name: Anthropology Graduate Student Organization
summary: AGSO supports the personal growth and professional development of Anthropology graduate students through fostering collegiality among graduate students, organizing networking events, inviting speakers and supporting grads attending academic conferences.

name: American Water Resources Association UGA Student Chapter
summary:
AWRA (American Water Resources Association) UGA Student Chapter 
Our mission is to advance multidisciplinary water resources education, management, and research on the UGA campus. We invite anyone interested in water to join.

name: Her Campus UGA
summary: Her Campus is the #1 online community written by college women for college women with content focused on style, beauty, life, love, career, health, and school.

name: Society of Entrepreneurs
summary: Society of Entrepreneurs is made of a community of like-minded change-makers with an entrepreneurial spirit. SOE connects students with opportunities to drive and inspire entrepreneurial change through our three pillars: to connect, inspire, and create.

name: Geography Graduate Student Association
summary: The purpose of the GGSA is to organize forums for the dissemination of geographic ideas and research within the department, UGA, and universities in the southeastern USA.

name: Georgia Students of Landscape Architecture
summary: To unite interested graduate and undergraduate landscape architecture, historic preservation, and urban planning students at the University of Georgia.

name: Turkish Student Association
summary: The Turkish Student Association at UGA celebrates Turkish culture and fosters community through cultural, educational, and social events. We welcome all UGA students interested in learning and connecting.

name: HOUSE
summary: HOUSE is a student organization aimed at the professional development of students through career coaching, leadership-development, and strategic networking with peers, faculty, and industry professionals.

name: Tax Law Society
summary: The Tax Law Society promotes the study of tax law and interest in the tax law profession through professional speakers, informational meetings, networking events, tax competitions, and community service with VITA.

name: Agricultural and Applied Economics Graduate Student Association
summary: Student-run social and academic club for Agricultural & Applied Economics graduate students at UGA.

name: CommunicAID+Nation
summary: CommunicAID+Nation raises money and awareness for individuals with communication disorders. We work with local Speech-Language Pathologists to provide communication devices to those in need. We are open to members of all majors and interests. Go Dawgs!

name: Environmental Law Association
summary: For those interested in how the power of the legal system is used to protect the environment.

name: Sigma Alpha Iota, Iota Zeta Chapter
summary: SAI is an International Music Fraternity for Women. Members are active in all areas of campus music and campus life. We work closely with people both on and off campus to further our musical endeavors, career goals, and our community.

name: Tau Sigma National Honor Society
summary: Tau Sigma National Honor Society at the University of Georgia is an academic honor society designed specifically to recognize and promote the academic excellence and involvement of transfer students.

name: Horticulture Club @ UGA, The
summary: We are the Horticulture Club at UGA! We learn about all things horticulture to grow and sell plants each semester while connecting our members with the ins and outs of the green industry! Meetings are on Tuesdays at 7pm.

name: Blue Key Honor Society
summary: Blue Key recognizes junior and senior men and women of outstanding character and ability who have achieved distinction for scholarship and recognition for service and leadership.

name: Robotics Club
summary: We are a group of motivated college students aimed at robotic development, research and competition. Our organization expands classroom learning through hands-on application and offers resources for STEM projects and interests.

name: DiGamma Kappa
summary: DGK provides members with networking opportunities, activities, and social functions within the broadcasting industry as well as an atmosphere to learn about future jobs and internships in the broadcasting realm, all while gaining hands-on experience.

name: Gamma Iota Sigma
summary: Gamma Iota Sigma is the international Risk Management, Insurance, and Actuarial Science fraternity.

name: Labor and Employment Law Association
summary: Provides a forum to discuss issues related to the area of labor and employment law and network among students and practitioners interested in this practice area

name: Health Law Society
summary: HLS offers members the opportunity to stay up to date on current issues in health law, to engage with other students interested in a career in health law, and to connect with practicing health law professionals.

name: Black Law Students Association
summary: The purpose of BLSA is to utilize the collective resources available to the chapter, including members, alumni and friends for the creation and maintenance of a law school environment that is conducive to Black students' effective study of the law.

name: Asian Law Students Association
summary: This organization is designed to create a strong Asian community at the law school and to increase awareness of issues Asians often face.

name: Rouge Magazine
summary: Rouge Magazine is the first fashion magazine on the University of Georgia's campus.

name: National Association of Black Journalists
summary: NABJ strives to bring awareness to issues concerning minority populations in the media industry. Our mission is to nurture relationships and interests in journalism and other media related fields while strengthening ties among black journalists on campus

name: H.O. Lund Entomology Club
summary: The H.O. Lund Club is a service- and social-oriented student organization  in the Department of Entomology, composed of anyone interested in the science of entomology.

name: Theta Tau
summary: A Professional Co-Ed Engineering Fraternity

name: Association for Computing Machinery
summary: Our mission is to help advance computer science as a profession, major, and community through local events and meetings.

name: Elections Committee
summary: The Elections Committee has oversight of the Student Government Association's elections process.

name: Society of Aspiring Plant Pathologists
summary: We endeavor to foster relations and engagement within the Department of Plant Pathology to improve program quality, enhance member satisfaction and career preparedness, and increase public awareness of the importance of plant pathology in modern society.

name: Bachelor of Social Work Club
summary: The Bachelor of Social Work Club at the UGA brings like-minded individuals together to educate, provide mentorships for, and promote the advancement of the next generation of helping professionals. We are open to all that are interested in social work.

name: Georgia Political Review
summary: Georgia Political Review is a student-run political magazine.

name: United States Institute of Theatre Technologies, Student Chapter at the University of Georgia
summary: This group aims to foster a free exchange of ideas and information regarding theatre technology and design; to engage in research and other projects; and to promote the stated purposes of United States Institute for Theatre Technologies.

name: Chemistry Graduate Student Organization
summary: The mission of CGSO will be to provide for the professional development needs of graduate students in chemistry and science at UGA.

name: Georgia Students for Public Administration
summary: The Georgia Students for Public Administration strive to help all students of public administration and policy at the University of Georgia build social and academic networks during and after their tenure at the University.

name: Thai Student Association
summary: The Thai Student Organization is an ethnic-cultural community club for the Thai community at UGA and those interested in Thai culture.

name: ICMA Student Chapter- UGA
summary: ICMA Student Chapter at UGA introduces and integrates students into the local government management profession, familiarizes students with ICMA, and provides resources and opportunities for students to connect with local government practitioners.

name: Bioinformatics Graduate Student Association
summary: BIGSA is a graduate student organization that promotes community and  collaboration in bioinformatics research at UGA.  Annually, we hold many social and educational events to support these goals.

name: Educational Theory & Practice Graduate Student Organization
summary: The ETAP GSO represents graduate students and provides networking, professional development, and cross-disciplinary study opportunities.

name: Ronald McDonald House Charities Club @UGA
summary: The Ronald McDonald House Charities Club provides volunteering and fundraising opportunities that support programs that directly improve the health and well-being of children and their families.

name: Education Abroad Ambassadors
summary: The purpose/mission of the Education Abroad Ambassadors (EAA) serves to promote and support UGA students interested in education abroad experiences.

name: Breaking the Shackles: Athens
summary: Leveraging music as a platform to illuminate slavery. Creating a community of advocates to advance freedom.

name: Statistics Club
summary: We build communion among the diverse members of the statistical community, promote scholarship and training through colloquia and seminars, and sponsor the development of statistics classrooms in and out of UGA through financial/technological donations.

name: Chamber Music Society
summary: Chamber Music Society is dedicated to providing ensembles and performance opportunities to musicians of all majors.

name: Black Pre-Law Student Association
summary:
The purpose of the Black Pre-Law Student Association (BPLSA) is to cultivate and foster community
amongst black students at the University of Georgia through motivation, mentorship, support, and
networking from various professionals. Membership is open.

name: National Council of Negro Women, University of Georgia Section
summary: NCNW at UGA aims to lead, develop, and advocate for Black women as they pursue their higher education. We accomplish this through programs focused on empowerment, unity, and service in the University of Georgia and Athens-Clarke County communities.

name: Romance Languages Graduate Student Organization
summary: The main goal of ROML-GSO is to create a space in which graduate students in the Department of Romance Languages can exchange scholarly and creative ideas as well receive mentorship and training from the experienced faculty in the department.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi - Alpha Epsilon
summary: Alpha Kappa Psi is a nationally recognized co-ed professional fraternity. Members participate in professional, social, and philanthropic events.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
summary: SHPE changes lives by empowering the Hispanic community to realize its fullest potential and to impact the world through STEM awareness, access, support, and development. Membership is extended to all students.

name: Honors Student Council
summary: HSC is comprised of roughly 30 students dedicated to enhancing the Honors experience and engaging their peers in the Honors community. HSC hosts a wide variety events aimed to provide social experiences and stimulate academic curiosity.

name: Developmental Biology Graduate Student Association
summary: The DBGSA seeks to act as a voice and support system for graduate students looking to enhance or focus their studies in the field of Developmental Biology.

name: Energy Concept @ UGA, The
summary: Energy Concept @ UGA is a student-run organization dedicated to the interdisciplinary exploration of energy across business, policy, and technology.

name: Horticulture Graduate Student Association
summary: The Horticulture Graduate Student Association promotes social events, academic discussions, professional development training, and outdoor activities for its members.

name: Breast Cancer Awareness Club
summary: BCAC promotes breast cancer awareness through education, outreach, and support. We empower students to engage in prevention efforts, advocate for early detection, and provide a compassionate space for those impacted, fostering lasting community impact.

name: Dairy Science Club
summary: DSC gathers people with different backgrounds but similar interests in an effort to build friendships and gain familiarity with the dairy industry. We are dedicated to immersing our members to all aspects of the industry and bonding over a love of cows!

name: Graduate Pride
summary: We are a group for graduate and professional students at UGA who identify as LGBTQIA+ to build community.

name: Exercise is Medicine at UGA
summary: Exercise is Medicine at UGA aims to educate and offer programs to empower members of the UGA campus community and surrounding communities to engage in behaviors that promote health and wellbeing.

name: Sri Lankan Student Association
summary: The Sri Lankan Student Association is an organization at the University of Georgia that provide an opportunity to UGA community to explore the Sri Lankan History, Tradition, and Culture through events and communications with Sri Lankan community at UGA.

name: Nepalese Student Association
summary:
NSA is formed by the current graduate and undergraduate students at UGA, who are 
originally from Nepal. The primary objective of the organization is to unite the Nepalese community in Athens so that we can share our cultures and traditions.

name: Sigma Tau Delta
summary: We are the English Honors Society at the University of Georgia. Sigma Tau Delta confers distinction for high achievement in English language and literature in undergraduate, graduate, and professional studies

name: Biochemistry Undergraduate Society
summary: Join BUS for biochemistry major-related social events, activities, professional opportunities, and engagement with the biochemistry community! All STEM students and faculty are welcome to join BUS or attend events!

name: Lambda Chi Alpha
summary: We inspire and equip men to lead an ethical life of growth, service and leadership. Lambda Chi Alpha envisions a world where the ideal man pursues a life of respect and inclusion for all. A world where we support and encourage one another.

name: Sigma Phi Epsilon
summary: Sigma Phi Epsilon Fraternity is a student group that bases its foundation around three cardinal principles, Virtue, Diligence, and Brotherly Love in an effort to ensure that all members achieve the most beneficial experience possible while at UGA.

name: BOLD Modeling Agency
summary: BOLD Modeling Agency is an organization started by POC students with the goal of celebrating, molding, and empowering unrepresented faces in the fashion model scene both on and off campus.

name: Dodd Ambassadors
summary: The Dodd Ambassadors are a select group of students dedicated to enhancing the image of and interest in the Lamar Dodd School of Art at the University of Georgia

name: Partners in Health: Engage
summary: PIH Engage is a nationwide network of teams that support the work of Partners in Health, a global health non-profit. Our University of Georgia chapter strives to build a strong local community, advocate politically, and fundraise.

name: American Society of Law, Medicine, & Ethics
summary: The mission of the American Society of Law, Medicine, & Ethics shall be to provide high-quality scholarship, debate, and critical thought for undergraduate students at the intersection of law, medicine, and ethics.

name: DECA
summary: DECA prepares emerging leaders and entrepreneurs for careers in marketing, finance, hospitality and management in colleges around the globe.

name: Kappa Kappa Psi - Kappa Mu Chapter
summary: The Kappa Mu Chapter is a brotherhood dedicated to the University of Georgia bands through service, support, leadership and appreciation for music.

name: Themed Entertainment Association
summary: The Themed Entertainment Association (TEA) is an organization for creators of theme parks, interactive experiences, museums, restaurants, and more. It encompasses design, engineering, hospitality, and hundreds of other fields.

name: Backpack Project of Athens
summary: TBP/ATH is a student-run 501©(3) nonprofit organization that serves the homeless community in Athens, Georgia. We aim to engage with the Athens homeless population through volunteer programs, fundraising, and raising awareness.

name: National Alliance on Mental Illness on Campus UGA
summary: The National Alliance on Mental Illness (NAMI) on Campus UGA is a mental health organization on campus, and as a part of the larger national organization, our mission is to provide advocacy, education, support, and public awareness about mental health.

name: Mary Frances Early College of Education Student Ambassadors
summary: The MFECOE Student Ambassadors represent the students of the Mary Frances Early College of Education. We help connect students to resources, help host official programming, and provide input to college administration in order to improve student life.

name: Law Student Chapter of the Federal Bar Association at UGA Law
summary: To represent the Federal Bar Association to law students, to stimulate the interest in activities of FBA, to conduct programs of interest and value, and to assist the establishment, improvement and coordination of active student organizations in FBA.

name: Women in Finance
summary: Women in Finance's mission is to educate, empower, and unite a network of students endeavoring to create long lasting professional relationships and careers in the finance field.

name: Athens Prison Tutorial
summary: Athens Prison Tutorial serves the interests of incarcerated peoples in Athens-Clarke County through regular educational programming. This includes GED tutoring, SAT/ACT prep, and other forms of education deemed desirable or necessary.

name: Women Pre-Law Students Association
summary: WPSA promotes a driven atmosphere of pre-law students. We shall provide our members with resources, mentors, and professionals to guide their undergraduate to a career in law.

name: Turfgrass Management Club at UGA
summary: The objective of the Turfgrass Management Club at UGA is to focus on the mentoring, networking, and overall education of Turfgrass Management students in the Department of Crop and Soil Sciences.

name: Society of Professional Journalists at UGA
summary: The Society of Professional Journalists at UGA is a student chapter of the national chapter of SPJ which provides resources to aspiring journalists and media communicators. We hold monthly meetings and even attend the national conference every fall!

name: Lambda Theta Phi Latin Fraternity, Inc.
summary: We are a service/social multicultural fraternity. We strive to promote academic excellence, professionalism, and brotherhood. We are a nonexclusive and we welcome interested college males from all different backgrounds.

name: Regenerative Bioscience Center Graduate Student Association
summary: The Regenerative Bioscience Center Graduate Student Association (RBC GSA) aims to unite RBC professional students through professional development, social, and community outreach events.

name: Board Game Club at UGA
summary: If you love board games, but don't have the time to organize a game, don't have the people to play, or just want to try different games you've never had the chance to play before -- then this is the club for you! All experience levels and games welcome!

name: Actuaries at UGA
summary: This organization is open to students of all majors considering a career in Actuarial Science. By hosting guest speakers, networking events and presentations, Actuaries at UGA aims to educate potential actuaries on how to succeed in the profession.

name: Pre-Optometry Club
summary: This organization is dedicated to informing students interested in the Pre-optometry track more about the profession as well as educating them on the optometry school application process. It also serves as a way for these students to socialize.

name: Philosophy Undergraduate Student Association
summary: An undergraduate student organization that sits in a circle at 6 pm every Tuesday in Peabody room 220 and discusses a topic. No philosophy knowledge or experience is required! The aim is to foster clear communication and an intellectual appreciation.

name: Bengali Cultural Organization
summary: Bengali Cultural Organization (BCO) serves UGA community members of Bengali origin and anyone who identifies with the region culturally or socially. It offers the opportunity for students to be exposed to the culture and learn more about it.

name: National Lawyers Guild
summary: NLG seeks to unite the lawyers, law students, legal workers, and jailhouse lawyers of America in an organization that is an effective political and social force in the service of the people and holds human rights more sacred than property interests.

name: Clarke County Mentors at UGA
summary: Clarke County Mentors provides support for students mentoring with the Clarke County Mentor Program. These volunteers serve as mentors to students in all of the Athens-Clarke County public schools, ranging from kindergarten to twelfth grade.

name: Nerd Culture Club
summary: NCC is a social group whose members get together to casually play and appreciate video games, board games, Japanese entertainment, comic books, trading card games, and much more.

name: Global Health Union
summary: Our organization strives to facilitate campus-wide engagement and interdisciplinary collaboration in global health initiatives outside the classroom.

name: Best Buddies at UGA
summary: Best Buddies is a nonprofit organization dedicated to establishing one-to-one friendships between people with intellectual and developmental disabilities and their peers.

name: Girls in Healthcare
summary: While promoting and recognizing the importance of humility, intentionality, and passion in the pursuit of the healthcare profession, Girls in Healthcare strives to unite all pre-health students on campus through mentorship, service, and fellowship

name: American Society For Microbiology Student Chapter
summary: Your participation in the ASM Student Chapter is a great way to combine community involvement, professional development, and an interest in microbes. Join ASM to connect with other students, faculty, and researchers who also love microbiology.

name: Women in Media
summary: Women in Media strives to foster a community of students interested in diverse forms of media, including journalism, public relations, advertising, entertainment and media studies, film studies and marketing.

name: Chinese Language and History Learning Association
summary: CLHLA advocates Chinese language and history to children and teens.

name: College of Engineering Student Ambassadors
summary: The Engineering Student Ambassadors work to build awareness of the UGA College of Engineering on campus and across the state. Ambassadors help promote pride in Bulldog Engineering.

name: Student Veterans Resource Center
summary: The Student Veterans Resource Center (SVRC) serves as a central location for information about the university’s services for student veterans while also offering support, advocacy and a convenient location for student veterans to gather throughout the d

name: Student Conduct
summary: As a department within the Division of Student Affairs, the mission of Student Conduct is to enhance the learning environment for members of the University of Georgia community.

name: Disability Resource Center
summary: The DRC strives to promote a welcoming academic, physical, and social environment for students with disabilities at UGA.

name: Armed Forces Association at UGA School of Law
summary: Serving veterans and other law students interested in the military to promote networking, law student support, and discourse about the intersection of law and national security.

name: Music Business Ambassadors
summary: Music Business Ambassadors provides opportunities for students interested in music business to network, represent, and engage with MBUS resources through events and firsthand experience.

name: Physics Graduate Student Association
summary: An organization dedicated to improving the graduate experience of physics students by fostering a sense of community and engagement within the student body and the UGA at large.

name: Entertainment Media Industries Club
summary: The Industry is a networking space for future filmmakers to collaborate on future projects, recruit crew, learn about set safety, and develop ideas for screenplays.

name: Genetic Education Network and Experience Society
summary: G.E.N.E.S. was founded to build connections between all students who are interested in the field of genetics. We host speakers from a wide variety of industries including medicine, academia, and law to help students make informed career decisions.

name: BrainIAC
summary: The goal of BrainIAC is to increase brain injury awareness while providing support and resources to help affected students cope with negative impacts such injuries may have on academics and quality of life.

name: Black Business Student Association
summary: It is a resource to African-Americans and/or people of color catering to both intended, current and Alumni of the Terry College of Business. We seek to encourage academic, personal and professional development in our members.

name: Planned Parenthood Generation Action
summary: This club provides sexual and health education and resources to students. Encouraging education on reproduction and advocating for reproductive justice.

name: RISE
summary: RISE provides UGA's rural students with a sense of community on campus through mentorship, outreach, social events, and optional community service opportunities.

name: Women in Economics
summary: Women in Economics is a Terry student organization dedicated to providing academic, personal, and professional development events to students.

name: School of Law First-Generation Student Association
summary: The School of Law’s First-Generation Student Association (FGSA) aims to enhance the academic success, professional growth and personal development of First-Generation Students at the University of Georgia School of Law.

name: Tau Beta Pi Engineering Honor Society
summary: Tau Beta Pi Engineering Honor Society is the only engineering honor society at the University of Georgia which seeks to recognize and promote the academic success of the College of Engineering's students.

name: Girls Who Code
summary: Members visit local elementary, middle, and high schools weekly to create an enjoyable, supportive, and challenging environment for young girls and introduce them to the world of computer science and STEM fields.

name: Biotechnology Club
summary: Biotechnology Club focuses on connecting students interested in biotechnology, but also introducing students who may not know much about biotechnology to the topic.

name: Center for Tropical and Emerging Global Diseases Graduate Student Association
summary: The Center for Tropical and Emerging Global Diseases Graduate Student Association aims to provide a means for CTEGD graduate students to interact and to provide a network for discussion and improvement of student’s development and career goals.

name: National Hispanic Pharmacist Association
summary:
The goals of NHPA are to define the future role of minority health professionals in the community,
develop a positive image of minority health professionals, and coordinate programs that will improve the healthcare of underserved Hispanic populations.

name: American Statistical Association Student Chapter
summary: The ASA Student Chapter is an organization for students interested in statistics that will focus on professional development, outside learning, and service.

name: Terry Fintech Society
summary: The purpose for Terry FinTech Society is to disseminate and further explore the emerging areas of FinTech in order to adequately prepare the next generation of innovators and problem-solvers.

name: Common Ground
summary: Common Ground is a club created for the purpose of showing students who don't share the same political ideologies that they have more in common than they might think. We participate in debates in order to find common ground between opposing ideologies.

name: Sustainable Business Society
summary: Sustainable Business Society aims to equip members with tools to analyze and better understand the intersection between business and sustainability.

name: Chi Epsilon Pi Atmospheric Sciences Honor Society
summary: The organization description of ΧΕΠ at UGA is to recognize students for achieving academic excellence in the UGA Atmospheric Sciences Program through a forum for discussion, public outreach services, and guest speakers.

name: Loch Johnson Society
summary: The Loch Johnson Society is a student-led, non-partisan foreign policy group at UGA that aims to shape future world leaders and practitioners by engaging in dialogue surrounding international security and trade.

name: University Transitions
summary: The Department of University Transitions provides dedicated support for key times of student transition.

name: Aurora at UGA
summary: Aurora at UGA serves to bring back joy into the lives of children with cancer and their siblings through heartening programs designed to put a smile on their faces amid challenging times.

name: Office of Service-Learning
summary: The OSL was established in 2005 to promote and support the development of quality academic service-learning experiences in response to critical community needs.

name: CodeHub
summary: CodeHub provides a space to explore coding, connect students to others passionate about coding, and provide students with additional resources for learning about coding and computer science.

name: First Gen Dawgs
summary: First Gen Dawgs is an organization for first-generational college undergraduates at the University of Georgia. This organization will create community, build students up professionally, and uplift the first-generational student population.

name: Beyond the Bell Tutoring
summary: Beyond the Bell is a UGA-led non-profit offering free STEM tutoring to Athens-Clarke high school students. Our volunteers help improve learning outcomes and inspire students to excel in their education.

name: Graduate Arts Community Council
summary: GACC's purpose is to build community within the graduate art programs.

name: Cognitive Science Club
summary: An interdisciplinary student organization exploring historical and contemporary theories of mind.

name: African Graduate Students' Forum at UGA
summary: The African Graduate Students' Forum (AGSF)  is a network of graduate students at UGA, established to promote African culture, and cater to the social and professional growth of its members. Membership is open to everyone.

name: PhD Consulting Club
summary: PhD Consulting Club aims to provide a means for students at UGA and its affiliated community to prepare themselves for careers in consulting and to create a platform for members to connect with UGA's network of consulting professionals.

name: Association of Black Psychologist Student Circle
summary: ABPsi Student Circle members are undergraduate and graduate students in psychology or a related discipline. Student Circle members have access to scholarship opportunities, leadership and professional development and student support.

name: Athens Student Investors Club
summary: Athens Student Investors Club is an organization focused on the stock market, global economy, and current events. Our main priority, however, is being a place where students with common interest can network and discuss polarizing topics.

name: Out in STEM
summary: A student organization that seeks to promote professional, social, and academic development for members by developing and facilitating a community for LGBT+ students and their allies in the STEM fields.

name: Game Builders Club
summary: Game Builders Club is an organization centered around video game development. Students of almost any background, art, music, programming, or even no design experience at all, are welcome to come learn, teach, and collaborate on video game projects.

name: Office of Sustainability
summary: The UGA Office of Sustainability coordinates, communicates, and advances initiatives in teaching, research, service, student engagement, and campus operations.

name: South Asian Law Students Association
summary: We hope to provide a means for students from the South Asian region, or of South Asian descent, or of interest in South Asian culture, at UGA Law and other universities, to meet and interact, and for advice on being a minority in the legal profession.

name: Gift of Life Marrow Registry
summary: The Gift of Life is a bone marrow and stem cell registry with several chapters in universities across the nation. Through hosting donor drives, information sessions, and community events, we aim to register as many members of our community as possible.

name: Choral Project
summary: The UGA Choral Project is an auditioned choir that seeks to imitate the "project" model of choral singing, and provide our students a near-professional experience.

name: Elevate Mentoring
summary: Elevate's mission is to create a firm foundation for young, African American students through mentorship by UGA students in order to foster academic and personal achievement and provide students with role models to look up to.

name: Cancer Support Community at UGA
summary: As the largest professionally led nonprofit network of cancer support worldwide, the Cancer Support Community is dedicated to ensuring that all people impacted by cancer are empowered by knowledge, strengthened by action, and sustained by community.

name: AccessPoint
summary: To work with UGA and the surrounding areas to promote inclusivity while advocating with and on behalf of  students and individuals with intellectual, physical, developmental, and mental disabilities in all Georgia communities.

name: Motorcycle Club of UGA®
summary: A community of fellow two wheel enthusiasts who want to better their riding ability as well as maximize their motorcycle safety. We are a group of motorcyclists of all kinds that venture together through our motorcycle journeys.

name: QStone
summary: QStone is a social organization that strives to create a fun, inclusive, safe, and comfortable environment for Black, Indigenous, and POC LGBTQ+ students of the University of Georgia.

name: Tabletop Wargaming at UGA
summary: We are a club dedicated to fostering a community of people that are interested in the hobby of tabletop wargaming and its facets. This includes painting models, playing the games, and socialization among players.

name: Transactional Law Club
summary: The Transactional Law Club seeks to inform law students on transactional-related careers they might not have considered otherwise. TLC will host panels, general body meetings, and networking events to connect students interested in this career field.

name: Interdisciplinary Field People
summary: Promoting the Interdisciplinary Field Program and sharing in local adventures!

name: Odum School of Ecology
summary: We study the fundamental ecological processes that shape the world, inspire and train future generations, and create science-based solutions to environmental problems.

name: Corsairs Law Society
summary: Corsairs Law Society is an organization of law students who have found jobs in business law outside of Georgia. The members of Law Corsairs provide advice to rising 2Ls and 3Ls who are interested in finding out-of-state jobs.

name: Georgia MBA Veterans Club
summary: Georgia MBA Veterans Club is a student Veteran organization within the Terry College of Business Graduate school that will support initiatives within the Military student population for Full-Time, Professional, and Executive MBA's (FTMBA, PMBA, EMBA).

name: Morehead Honors College
summary: The Morehead Honors College seeks to inspire students not only to meet but to exceed their academic dreams, and to provide them the means to do so.

name: Athens Poker
summary: Athens Poker is a collection of UGA students that meet once a week to play FREE Texas NLH tournaments/ring games together. We are dedicated to fostering a deeper understanding of the game, whether you are a complete beginner or an experienced player!

name: Perfect Pair
summary: Perfect Pair is created in order to establish one-on-one connections between seniors in assisted living communities and college students. Student volunteers and senior participants will be paired based on similar interests, hobbies, and backgrounds.

name: Minorities in Communication Sciences and Disorders
summary: M-CMSD strives to uplift and empower minority voices through community and advocacy to create diverse, inclusive, and culturally responsive spaces. We welcome all--minorities and allies!

name: Georgia Blacks in Business Graduate Association
summary: An organization founded to create space for intellectual apolitical interactions and dialogue to facilitate conversations that incorporate all voices in the discussion of business on a local, national, and global stage.

name: Equal Education for Athens
summary: The purpose of Equal Education for Athens is to provide free one-on-one tutoring in Mathematics and Reading to primary and secondary school students within Athens-Clarke County

name: Scholars of Finance
summary: National organization focused on ethical finance. Our goal is to inspire character and integrity in the finance leaders of tomorrow. Ultimately we hope this creates a future where all finance leaders steward the world's capital to serve the greater good.

name: Equity Research Society
summary: ERS produces Equity Research reports and aims to provide students with relevant experience in the field.

name: Popcorn Club
summary: We host weekly movie nights at MLC! Members of the club can nominate a movie they want to see, and everyone may vote on which movie they want to watch! We also allow enough time after the movie to discus our thoughts on the film. Join us today!

name: Youth Movement Against Alzheimer's
summary: To promote understanding of Alzheimer’s among youth and young adults by providing opportunities to help those affected by the disease.The vision of YMAA is to create a culture where youth embrace aging and alleviate the impact of Alzheimer’s.

name: Alexander Hamilton Society at UGA
summary: The Alexander Hamilton Society is a preprofessional international affairs club with the mission to launch students into foreign policy and national security careers.

name: Mahjong at UGA
summary: This club aims to provide a welcoming environment for UGA students, faculty, and staff to meet, interact, and play/appreciate the strategic game of Mahjong

name: Audiology Dogs
summary: AuD Dogs serves to establish a professional interest among UGA students in the career path of audiology, through monthly speakers! We strive to raise money for the speech and hearing clinic as well as create an alumni network for students!

name: Office of Inclusive Excellence
summary: We are committed to a vision of leadership in embracing diversity and inclusion and our staff prioritizes initiatives that serve the diversity goals and academic mission of the university.

name: Environmental Economics and Management Club
summary: Environmental Economics and Management Club will hold events to build a network with fellow Environmental Economics and Management Majors, and it will also work to foster academic and career development.

name: Physiology and Pharmacology Graduate Student Association
summary: The Physiology and Pharmacology Graduate Student Association is a casual organization for graduate students interested in physiology and pharmacology. Primary obligations include social events and networking across programs.

name: College of Family and Consumer Sciences
summary: The UGA College of Family and Consumer Sciences is a close-knit college known for its student-first approach. We focus on helping people thrive.

name: Career Center
summary: The Career Center provides a wide variety of services to UGA students and alumni, including Major & Career Exploration, Resume & Cover Letter Critiques, Job & Internship Searching, and much more.

name: Turning Point USA at UGA
summary: Turning Point USA’s mission is to educate students about the importance of fiscal responsibility, free markets, and constitutional rights through innovative campus activism and non-partisan, thought-provoking discussion.

name: Odum School Undergraduate Mentor Program
summary: The Odum School Undergraduate Mentor Program (OSUMP) pairs undergraduate students who are new to the Odum School with more senior ecology undergraduate majors to provide advice, mentoring, and community connections.

name: Arts Council
summary: The Arts Council works to foster an awareness and appreciation of the arts and an environment conducive to artistic innovation.

name: Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law and Government Fraternity
summary: Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law and Government Fraternity at UGA is a community of individuals interested in pursuing careers within the legal and government fields. We are committed to providing our brothers with the tools necessary to succeed in these fields.

name: Women's Network at UGA
summary: The Women’s Network empowers ambitious women looking to grow professionally while being lifted by their community, offering opportunities to gain exposure to the professional world and network with high profile speakers and peers. All are welcome!

name: Women in Political Science and International Affairs
summary: Meeting biweekly to provide a means for women majoring in Political Science and International Affairs to learn about career opportunities, interests, and discuss and give advice to aid in these goals. This organization is open to all students.

name: Film and Media Scoring Student Association
summary: If you like film music, we have a place for you! Come join us and learn about writing music for film, games, and TV with many guest artists and our own on faculty film composer.

name: Women in Cybersecurity
summary: WiCyS is dedicated to helping build a strong, gender-diverse cybersecurity workforce through the recruitment, retainment, and advancement of women in cybersecurity.

name: Catalyst
summary: Catalyst will allow students to go through the entire peer-review process and gain valuable writing experience through submitting manuscripts for publication in a student-led journal showcasing STEM undergraduates.

name: Undergraduate Research Club
summary: URC strives to provide a means for undergraduate students to learn about different aspects of research in academic and industrial capacities and to highlight opportunities for students to get involved in research at UGA and outside the university.

name: Between the Lines
summary: Between the Lines is dedicated to raising awareness about the media censorship epidemic in America; through community building and involvement, discussion and awareness of controversial topics, and focusing on one "banned" book a month.

name: Office for Student Success and Achievement
summary: The Office for Student Success and Achievement serves as a student success unit on campus. We provide a variety of services to help students transition into higher education and sustain their progress through the University’s unique academic environment.

name: Backlight Student Film Festival of Athens
summary: The Backlight Student Film Festival of Athens is an organization devoted to fostering innovation and creativity within the Georgia film community by promoting and highlighting the work of aspiring student filmmakers.

name: Space Research Club
summary: The Space Research Club serves as an organization to provide students with the opportunity to conduct research in aerospace.

name: Risefree
summary: Risefree is a student-led campaign organization advocating for college affordability and students' basic needs.

name: Georgia Museum of Natural History Student Association
summary: GMNH Club serves as a bridge between the Georgia Museum of Natural History and the student body of UGA. The goal of the club is to strengthen knowledge of the museum across campus, increase visitation, and attract student volunteers.

name: Law and Political Economy Collective at Georgia
summary: LPE meets on a regular basis to discuss articles and research pertaining to law and the political economy. Any member is welcome to facilitate these discussions. We also seek out active partnerships with other groups on campus.

name: Club Management Association of America Student Chapter
summary: The CMAA's objective is to promote relationships between club management professionals and other similar professions; to encourage the education and advancement of members, and to provide the resources needed for efficient and successful club operations.

name: Minorities in Business
summary: Minorities in Business is an organization dedicated to empowering underrepresented voices in the business world. Through networking, professional development, and social events, we strive to create a supportive community where members can succeed.

name: Office of Global Engagement
summary: The Office of Global Engagement provides experiential learning opportunities and resources for students to expand their resume and global experience through various programs – sending students to 88 countries and counting.

name: DevDogs
summary: A community of student developers, designers, and tech enthusiasts taking impactful projects from concept to completion.

name: Society of Environmental and Agronomic Developing Scientists
summary: An organization for anyone who is interested in Crop and Soil Sciences. We will hold monthly meetings as well as social events and workshops to foster academic and profession growth, student support, and community in our department.

name: National Residence Hall Honorary
summary: The National Residence Hall Honorary (NRHH) is a leadership-based organization comprised of exemplary students advocating for campus service and recognition. NRHH serves under University Housing and as a sibling to the Residence Hall Association (RHA).

name: NEST Run Club
summary: We’re a student-led run club built around intentional growth. We make running something students want to show up for, improving health, meeting people, and building real friendships in a welcoming, social environment that encourages consistency.

name: Free Time Finance
summary: Free Time Finance is a student-run organization at the University of Georgia that fosters excitement and engagement with personal finance topics, especially regarding college living.

name: Happy Little Accidents
summary: Happy Little Accidents is a club designed to unite artists and amateurs to destress from life's chaos. We will follow along with the painting tutorials of Bob Ross in a loose, fun format meant to brighten people's days instead of swamp them.

name: New Student Orientation
summary: New Student Orientation at the University of Georgia seeks to prepare students for academic and social engagement as members of the UGA community.

name: History Graduate Student Association
summary: Organization representing the History Department graduate students at UGA.

name: Terry College of Business
summary: Founded in 1912, the C. Herman and Mary Virginia Terry College of Business is steeped in a tradition of providing cutting-edge business education.

name: Tribute, The
summary: The Tribute is a student-led production club based upon the application of the age-old addage: “What is cinema?” The Tribute studies a filmmaker’s work and goes on to create a short based upon their specific style from pre-production to post-production.

name: Business Pre-Law Society
summary: The goal of BPLS is to educate students in business, which is a non-traditional pre-law major, about law school, including the admissions process, life in law school, and what life in corporate law looks like.

name: School of Public and International Affairs
summary: SPIA prepares undergraduate and graduate students for good citizenship and careers in public life and trains future generations of teachers and scholars in the fields of international affairs, political science, and public administration and policy.

name: AI Society at Terry
summary: A student-led organization dedicated to exploring artificial intelligence through workshops, guest speakers, and collaborative projects, fostering professional development and community engagement among members passionate about AI.

name: Knox Martin Foundation at UGA
summary: Our organization partners with The Knox Martin Foundation to raise awareness and funds for Glioblastoma brain cancer research at Duke University.

name: Little Legal Minds
summary: LLM educates Athens youth on their legal rights and connects them to accessible resources. Through interactive sessions, students learn key legal principles and how they apply to daily life, offered in after-school or classroom settings.

name: College of Environment and Design
summary: o The College of Environment + Design (CED) at the University of Georgia is one of the top institutions in the U.S. for landscape architecture, environmental design, and historic preservation.

name: Music Production Club
summary: Music Production Club is designed for learning and meeting other like-minded people who are passionate about music production, sound design, audio engineering, songwriting, and other production techniques!

name: Reconnect
summary:
We seek to create fully present, uninterrupted, social spaces where connection naturally
thrives at the University of Georgia by removing the barriers of technology.

name: South Asian and Asian Business Association
summary: To establish an organization that serves as the primary connection for all and any individuals, regardless of background, who appreciate and celebrate Asian Culture and have a desire to grow professionally in business. We welcome anyone to attend a GBM!

name: Immigration Law Society
summary: The Immigration Law Society provides an excellent opportunity for students to connect with immigration attorneys, learn more about immigration law and current policies, network with other students, and stay informed with up-to-date immigration news.

name: Vertically Integrated Projects for Research Society
summary: VIPRS at UGA fosters community and academic growth among students in the VIP Research Program and other students with similar interests. We hold research discussions to promote outreach, collaboration, and engagement in research across disciplines.

name: Business Brigades
summary: Business Brigades is an international movement providing economic opportunities in under-resourced areas in Ghana, Belize, Greece, and Panama by consulting micro-enterprises, assessing loan capacity payments, and providing financial literacy education.

name: NASA Technology Transfer University Program
summary: The NASA Technology Transfer University (T2U) Program at the University of Georgia is dedicated to integrating NASA-developed technologies into academic learning, fostering innovation, entrepreneurship, and workforce development.

name: Forensic Science Society
summary: The purpose of the Forensic Science Society shall be to educate students in forensic sciences, integrate different departments on campus, and connect students with other programs and resources. We will have guest speakers, fun labs, and field trips.

name: National Society of Black Women in Medicine
summary: The University of Georgia chapter of the National Society of Black Women in Medicine aims to increase the recruitment & retention of Black women pursuing careers in medicine, while simultaneously promoting the advancement of those already established.

name: Science in Film
summary: We watch interesting science fiction films and then discuss the legitimacy of the scientific experiments/logic

name: First Generation Law Society
